setup: Why don't scientists trust atoms?
punchline: Because they make up everything!

setup: What do you call a fake noodle?
punchline: An impasta!

setup: Why did the scarecrow win an award?
punchline: He was outstanding in his field!

setup: What do you call a bear with no teeth?
punchline: A gummy bear!

setup: Why don't eggs tell jokes?
punchline: They'd crack each other up!

setup: What did the ocean say to the beach?
punchline: Nothing, it just waved.

setup: Why did the bicycle fall over?
punchline: Because it was two-tired!

setup: What do you call a dog that does magic?
punchline: A Labracadabrador!

setup: Why can't you give Elsa a balloon?
punchline: Because she will let it go!

setup: What did the coffee report to the police?
punchline: A mugging!

setup: Why do programmers prefer dark mode?
punchline: Because light attracts bugs!

setup: What do you call a fish without eyes?
punchline: A fsh!

setup: Why did the math book look so sad?
punchline: Because it had too many problems.

setup: What do you call cheese that isn't yours?
punchline: Nacho cheese!

setup: Why couldn't the leopard play hide and seek?
punchline: Because he was always spotted!

setup: What did one wall say to the other wall?
punchline: I'll meet you at the corner!

setup: Why do chicken coops only have two doors?
punchline: Because if they had four, they'd be chicken sedans!

setup: What do you call a snowman with a six-pack?
punchline: An abdominal snowman!

setup: Why did the golfer bring two pairs of pants?
punchline: In case he got a hole in one!

setup: What do you call a factory that makes okay products?
punchline: A satisfactory!

setup: Why do Java developers wear glasses?
punchline: Because they can't C#!

setup: What did the buffalo say to his son when he left for college?
punchline: Bison!

setup: Why don't skeletons fight each other?
punchline: They don't have the guts!

setup: What do you call a sleeping dinosaur?
punchline: A dino-snore!

setup: Why did the tomato turn red?
punchline: Because it saw the salad dressing!

setup: What's the best thing about Switzerland?
punchline: I don't know, but the flag is a big plus!

setup: Why did the invisible man turn down the job offer?
punchline: He couldn't see himself doing it!

setup: What do you call a belt made of watches?
punchline: A waist of time!

setup: Why do cows have hooves instead of feet?
punchline: Because they lactose!

setup: What did the grape do when he got stepped on?
punchline: He let out a little wine!

setup: Why did the PowerPoint presentation cross the road?
punchline: To get to the other slide!

setup: What do you call a can opener that doesn't work?
punchline: A can't opener!

setup: Why don't oysters share?
punchline: Because they're shellfish!

setup: What did Santa say when he walked through a cactus patch?
punchline: Ho ho ho!

setup: Why did the man put his money in the freezer?
punchline: He wanted cold hard cash!

setup: What do you call a pig that does karate?
punchline: A pork chop!

setup: Why did the computer go to the doctor?
punchline: Because it had a virus!

setup: What do you call a boomerang that won't come back?
punchline: A stick!

setup: Why did the cookie go to the hospital?
punchline: Because he felt crummy!

setup: What do you call an alligator in a vest?
punchline: An investigator!

setup: Why did the employee get fired from the calendar factory?
punchline: He took a couple of days off!

setup: What do you call a parade of rabbits hopping backwards?
punchline: A receding hare-line!

setup: Why don't Christmas trees knit?
punchline: Because they always drop their needles!

setup: What did one hydrogen atom say to the other?
punchline: I think I lost an electron. Are you positive?

setup: Why did the stadium get hot after the game?
punchline: All the fans left!

setup: What do you call a lazy kangaroo?
punchline: A pouch potato!

setup: Why did the developer go broke?
punchline: Because he used up all his cache!

setup: What did the sushi say to the bee?
punchline: Wasabi!

setup: Why did the photo go to jail?
punchline: Because it was framed!

setup: What do you call a train carrying bubblegum?
punchline: A chew-chew train!

setup: Why did the Oreo go to the dentist?
punchline: Because it lost its filling!

setup: What do computers snack on?
punchline: Microchips!

setup: Why can't you trust an atom?
punchline: They make up literally everything!

setup: What did the janitor say when he jumped out of the closet?
punchline: Supplies!

setup: Why did the turkey join a band?
punchline: Because it had the drumsticks!

setup: Why did the Gen Alpha kid bring a phone to the dinner table?
punchline: Because the food was bussin and they needed to get it on the gram. No cap.

setup: What did the sigma dad say to his son?
punchline: You're not on that grindset, you're on that blindset — you left your glasses at school again.

setup: Why did the kid say his math test had rizz?
punchline: Because it was giving him problems and he couldn't say no.

setup: My son told me I was an NPC.
punchline: I said "Have you tried turning me off and on again?" Same response every time.

setup: What's the difference between a Gen Alpha kid and a dictionary?
punchline: The dictionary's definition of "mid" doesn't hurt my feelings.

setup: My daughter said my cooking was "giving Ohio."
punchline: I said, "Well, at least Ohio has good taste — they invented the hot dog."

setup: Why did the skibidi toilet go to therapy?
punchline: It had too much brainrot and couldn't process anything.

setup: My kid said I had no aura.
punchline: I said, "Son, I had aura before it was called aura. We called it 'dad energy.'"

setup: What do you call a dad who tries to use Gen Alpha slang?
punchline: Delulu with a capital L. And the L isn't for "Lucid."

setup: My son said his sandwich was "bussin."
punchline: I said, "It should be — it just took the bus from the kitchen."

setup: Why did the Gen Alpha kid fail the driving test?
punchline: They tried to ratio the instructor instead of making a ratio-nal turn.

setup: I told my kids I was the main character.
punchline: They said I was more of a tutorial NPC that repeats the same three phrases.

setup: What did the dad say when his kid called him "sus"?
punchline: I'm not sus, I'm your father. Though I did just eat the last cookie... that IS kinda sus.

setup: My daughter said "slay" when I made breakfast.
punchline: I said, "I didn't slay anything, these eggs came from a very alive chicken."

setup: Why do Gen Alpha kids make bad poker players?
punchline: They can't stop saying "no cap" — they keep showing their hand.

setup: My son said he was "mewing" at the dinner table.
punchline: I said, "In my day we just called that 'chewing with your mouth closed.' You're welcome."

setup: I asked my kid what "fanum tax" means.
punchline: Now I finally have a word for what I've been doing to their fries for 15 years.

setup: What's a Gen Alpha kid's favorite type of music?
punchline: Whatever slaps. But not actual slapping — that's a timeout.

setup: My kid said "bet" when I told him to clean his room.
punchline: It's been 3 hours. I think we have different definitions of "bet."

setup: Why did the dad start looksmaxxing?
punchline: His kids said his fit was "giving 2005." He said, "That was a GREAT year."

setup: I told my daughter she was "ate" at her recital.
punchline: She said, "Dad, it's 'ate and left no crumbs.' You left ALL the crumbs."

setup: What did the Gen Alpha kid say about their dad's new car?
punchline: "It's giving... mid-life crisis." They're not wrong.

setup: My son said he was "cooking" on his homework.
punchline: I checked. He had the stove on and his textbook next to a pot. We need to talk.

setup: I tried to be sigma at the parent-teacher conference.
punchline: Turns out sitting alone in the corner not talking to anyone is called "concerning behavior" for adults.

setup: Why did the W go to school?
punchline: To avoid taking any more Ls. (My kids didn't laugh either. That's an L for me.)
